summary: REQ-15 and REQ-16 appear largely implemented: brand text updated in header/footer/hero and document title, and shimmer/glitter intensity increased via stronger shimmer utilities and applied to header/hero/cards/buttons. However, the diff also introduces multiple unrequested storefront/auth/admin expansions (not part of renaming/theme boost) that conflict with the stated non-goals.

hallucinated_features.description: Added/expanded jewelry storefront and admin management (new public jewelry pages and admin jewelry CRUD routes/hooks).
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest explicitly says not to add new storefront features beyond renaming and theme intensity updates; jewelry feature expansion is out of scope.
evidence: frontend-file-summaries.txt ("frontend/src/App.tsx": new public jewelry pages and admin jewelry management routes)

hallucinated_features.description: Introduced first-time user profile setup modal after Internet Identity login.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by REQ-15/REQ-16; also beyond rename/theme changes.
evidence: frontend-file-summaries.txt ("frontend/src/components/auth/ProfileSetupModal.tsx": modal dialog for first-time user profile setup)

hallucinated_features.description: Added/introduced admin route guard and access denied screen components.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by REQ-15/REQ-16; beyond rename/theme changes (even if related to auth, it is additional feature work).

hallucinated_features.description: Added new generic UI state components (EmptyState, etc.).
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by REQ-15/REQ-16; beyond rename/theme changes.

hallucinated_features.description: Changed footer to include/retain a caffeine.ai referral link with UTM tracking based on hostname/app identifier.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by REQ-15/REQ-16; could be considered an unrequested marketing/tracking adjustment (though not a third-party service integration per se).
evidence: frontend/src/components/layout/SiteFooter.tsx (caffeine.ai link with UTM parameters using window.location.hostname)
